title: Fair-Use Cache Keys, TTL, Clock Injection, Copies, and Eviction · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs fair-use cache keys, ttl, clock injection, copies, and eviction from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded TTL resource cache with observable decisions after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Canonicalize endpoint, resource identity, and representation into a typed cache key without mixing user spelling or presentation.
Inject a clock, define freshness at exact boundaries, and distinguish fetched, stored, observed, and expires times.
Choose immutable values, deep copies, or documented ownership so callers cannot mutate cached history or other results.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound entries and bytes, define eviction, expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, and bypass counters, and test each transition.

hints: For Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Cache Capacity E…, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Pokedex Go Cache Capacity Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Cache Capacity E…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 3459 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile canonical cache decisions, freshness, ownership, and capacity eviction; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound entries and bytes, define eviction, expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, and bypass counters, and test each transition.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Cache Capacity E…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A TTL automatically bounds cache memory.
